{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Health Economist",
  "description": "Nikao Support Initiative is an African-based NGO helping governments and communities to improve health outcomes by strengthening health systems, enhancing primary health care (PHC), and advancing sustainable healthcare financing.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Health Economist

Job Hiring Organization: Nikao Support Initiative

Job Location: Nigeria

Employment Type: Full-time/Hybrid

Job Summary


	We are seeking a highly skilled and experienced Health Economist to support research, financing analysis, and policy-driven health interventions.
	The successful candidate will play a key role in generating evidence-based insights that strengthen healthcare programmes, improve resource allocation, and inform strategic decision-making across the health sector.


Key Responsibilities


	Conduct health economics research and policy analysis.
	Lead cost-effectiveness, cost-benefit, and budget impact assessments.
	Support healthcare financing strategy development and resource optimization.
	Develop analytical models to inform programme and policy decisions.
	Prepare high-quality technical reports, presentations, and policy briefs.
	Provide advisory support on health technology assessment and costing studies.
	Analyse data and translate findings into actionable recommendations.
	Support proposal development, grant writing, and donor-funded initiatives where required.


Requirements


	PhD in Health Economics, Economics, or a related field preferred.
	Minimum of 5 years of experience applying health economics in Sub-Saharan Africa, particularly Nigeria.
	Master&rsquo;s degree with strong relevant research experience may be considered.
	Proven expertise in cost-effectiveness, cost-benefit, and budget impact analysis.
	Experience in health technology assessment and costing studies.
	Strong understanding of Nigerian health financing systems, including NHIA, state insurance schemes, and donor funding mechanisms.
	Proficiency in Stata, Python, or TreeAge is an advantage.
	Excellent report writing, data visualization, and presentation skills.
	Published research or peer-reviewed journal experience preferred.
	Prior experience working with international development organizations, research institutions, or donor-funded foundations is an added advantage.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their applications to: recruitment@bockaspreston.com using &quot;Application &ndash; Health Economist&quot; as the subject of the email.

Application Requirements
Interested candidates should submit:


	Updated CV (maximum 5 pages)
	Technical Statement (maximum 2 pages) outlining proposed analytical approach
	Two writing samples (reports, policy briefs, research papers, or related technical work)
	Names and contact details of two professional referees.
",
  "datePosted": "2026-05-16",

  },

Health Economist Job at Nikao Support Initiative




        
    

        
        Nikao Support Initiative is an African-based NGO helping governments and communities to improve health outcomes by strengthening health systems, enhancing primary health care (PHC), and advancing sustainable healthcare financing.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Health Economist

Job Hiring Organization: Nikao Support Initiative

Job Location: Nigeria

Employment Type: Full-time/Hybrid

Job Summary
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	We are seeking a highly skilled and experienced Health Economist to support research, financing analysis, and policy-driven health interventions.
	The successful candidate will play a key role in generating evidence-based insights that strengthen healthcare programmes, improve resource allocation, and inform strategic decision-making across the health sector.


Key Responsibilities


	Conduct health economics research and policy analysis.
	Lead cost-effectiveness, cost-benefit, and budget impact assessments.
	Support healthcare financing strategy development and resource optimization.
	Develop analytical models to inform programme and policy decisions.
	Prepare high-quality technical reports, presentations, and policy briefs.
	Provide advisory support on health technology assessment and costing studies.
	Analyse data and translate findings into actionable recommendations.
	Support proposal development, grant writing, and donor-funded initiatives where required.


Requirements


	PhD in Health Economics, Economics, or a related field preferred.
	Minimum of 5 years of experience applying health economics in Sub-Saharan Africa, particularly Nigeria.
	Master’s degree with strong relevant research experience may be considered.
	Proven expertise in cost-effectiveness, cost-benefit, and budget impact analysis.
	Experience in health technology assessment and costing studies.
	Strong understanding of Nigerian health financing systems, including NHIA, state insurance schemes, and donor funding mechanisms.
	Proficiency in Stata, Python, or TreeAge is an advantage.
	Excellent report writing, data visualization, and presentation skills.
	Published research or peer-reviewed journal experience preferred.
	Prior experience working with international development organizations, research institutions, or donor-funded foundations is an added advantage.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their applications to: [email protected] using "Application – Health Economist" as the subject of the email.

Application Requirements
Interested candidates should submit:


	Updated CV (maximum 5 pages)
	Technical Statement (maximum 2 pages) outlining proposed analytical approach
	Two writing samples (reports, policy briefs, research papers, or related technical work)
	Names and contact details of two professional referees.
    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Mobile Developer (React Native)",
  "description": "Vaste Holdings is building a regulated investment and savings platform for the Nigerian retail market &mdash; enabling individuals to onboard, fund a wallet, and access structured investment products through a secure, compliant digital experience.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Mobile Developer (React Native)
Job Location: Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

The role


	We are a growing asset management and fintech firm looking for a Cross-Platform Mobile Developer with good years of experience.
	Qualified candidate will build and maintain mobile applications for retail investors, portfolio tracking, and real-time asset management.


Key responsibilities


	Develop and maintain cross-platform mobile apps (iOS &amp; Android) using React Native.
	Build portfolio management dashboards showing asset allocation, returns, risk metrics, and transaction history.
	Integrate REST APIs for user authentication, trade execution, fund transfers, and market data.
	Implement secure storage, biometric login, certificate pinning, and encryption for fintech compliance.
	Optimize performance for large financial datasets (e.g., historical NAVs, price feeds, transaction logs).
	Collaborate in-office 2 days/week with backend engineers, product managers, and QA teams.


Required experience and skills Technical skills


	4-5 years of hands-on of professional mobile development experience.
	Strong proficiency in React Native (TypeScript).
	Proven experience in fintech, asset management, or wealth management (portfolio apps, investment dashboards, trading interfaces).
	Experience with state management (Redux, Bloc, Provider, or MobX).
	Familiarity with financial charts (MPAndroidChart, Victory Native, flchart).
	Knowledge of RESTful APIs, WebSockets (for live prices), and offline data persistence.
	Version control (Git) and CI/CD basics. &bull; Ability to work in a fast?paced but supportive fintech environment. Fintech and regulatory experience
	Working knowledge of SEC Nigeria regulations, fund management, or roboadvisory concepts.
	Experience with Nigerian fintech APIs (Flutterwave, Paystack, or banking gateways).
	Published apps on Google Play Store / Apple App Store.


Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should forward their Applications and referrals to: careers@vasteltd.com or matthew.adesina@vasteltd.com using the Position as the subject of the email.

Note


	Candidates should share their CV and a short note covering their most relevant fintech delivery experience.
	Where available, references from previous technical leadership engagements are strongly encouraged.
	Shortlisted candidates will be contacted for an initial conversation with the team.

  "validThrough": "2026-06-05",

  },

Mobile Developer (React Native) Job at Vaste Holdings




        
    

        
        Vaste Holdings is building a regulated investment and savings platform for the Nigerian retail market — enabling individuals to onboard, fund a wallet, and access structured investment products through a secure, compliant digital experience.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Mobile Developer (React Native)
Job Location: Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

The role
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	We are a growing asset management and fintech firm looking for a Cross-Platform Mobile Developer with good years of experience.
	Qualified candidate will build and maintain mobile applications for retail investors, portfolio tracking, and real-time asset management.


Key responsibilities


	Develop and maintain cross-platform mobile apps (iOS & Android) using React Native.
	Build portfolio management dashboards showing asset allocation, returns, risk metrics, and transaction history.
	Integrate REST APIs for user authentication, trade execution, fund transfers, and market data.
	Implement secure storage, biometric login, certificate pinning, and encryption for fintech compliance.
	Optimize performance for large financial datasets (e.g., historical NAVs, price feeds, transaction logs).
	Collaborate in-office 2 days/week with backend engineers, product managers, and QA teams.


Required experience and skills Technical skills


	4-5 years of hands-on of professional mobile development experience.
	Strong proficiency in React Native (TypeScript).
	Proven experience in fintech, asset management, or wealth management (portfolio apps, investment dashboards, trading interfaces).
	Experience with state management (Redux, Bloc, Provider, or MobX).
	Familiarity with financial charts (MPAndroidChart, Victory Native, flchart).
	Knowledge of RESTful APIs, WebSockets (for live prices), and offline data persistence.
	Version control (Git) and CI/CD basics. • Ability to work in a fast?paced but supportive fintech environment. Fintech and regulatory experience
	Working knowledge of SEC Nigeria regulations, fund management, or roboadvisory concepts.
	Experience with Nigerian fintech APIs (Flutterwave, Paystack, or banking gateways).
	Published apps on Google Play Store / Apple App Store.


Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should forward their Applications and referrals to: [email protected] or [email protected] using the Position as the subject of the email.

Note


	Candidates should share their CV and a short note covering their most relevant fintech delivery experience.
	Where available, references from previous technical leadership engagements are strongly encouraged.
	Shortlisted candidates will be contacted for an initial conversation with the team.
    

    

        Click here to Apply

	We are for looking for a logistics manager who knows the courier business from the inside out. The ideal candidate will be responsible for managing end-to-end logistics, ensuring timely deliveries, and improving operational efficiency across our distribution network.

	Proven ability to reduce late deliveries and operational costs.

	Proficiency in logistics software and tracking systems .
	Strong communication and problem- solving skills for fast paced environments.

N120,000 / Montn

Method of Application&nbsp;

Logistics Manager Job at Odixcity Consulting




        
    

        
        Odixcity Consulting is an International Hr consulting and procurement firm. With expertise in Recruitment & Selection, Performance management, Training & Development, Compensation and benefits as well as Outsourcing of goods and services for organizations.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Logistics Manager

Job Location: Port Harcourt, Rivers
Employment Type: Full-time (Onsite)

Job Summary
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	We are for looking for a logistics manager who knows the courier business from the inside out. The ideal candidate will be responsible for managing end-to-end logistics, ensuring timely deliveries, and improving operational efficiency across our distribution network.


Key Responsibilities


	Manage courier fleets (Bikes, vans, cars) for same day and scheduled deliveries.
	Optimize delivery routes to reduce costs and improve speed.
	Coordinate real-time dispatch and rerouting during disruptions.
	Track LPIs on time deliveries, RTO rates, cost per stop.
	Lead driver briefings, training, and performance reviews.
	Work with technology teams to improve TMS and tracking tools.


Requirements


	Candidates should possess a Bachelor's Degree with 2+ years in logistics management, specifically with courier or last-minute services.
	Hands on experience with route planning, POD systems, and real-time tracking
	Proven ability to reduce late deliveries and operational costs.
	Strong leadership and team management skill.
	Proficiency in logistics software and tracking systems .
	Strong communication and problem- solving skills for fast paced environments.


Salary
N120,000 / Montn

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Legal Assistant",
  "description": "Odixcity Consulting - Our client, a growing Practice, is recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Legal Assistant&nbsp;

Job Location: Port Harcourt, Rivers (Remote)
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	We are seeking a motivated, eloquent, and highly responsible Legal Assistant to join our growing practice.
	This role is ideal for an attorney who is hardworking, proactive, eager to learn, and ready to step into a leadership and supervisory position within the firm.
	As a legal Assistant you will handle assigned matters in immigration, personal injury/accident, and family law cases while also assisting with team supervision, client intake conversion, task review, and operational support.
	The ideal candidate must be able to undergo rigorous training, adapt quickly, and lead by example through professionalism, accountability, and strong work ethics.


Responsibilities


	Handle assigned immigration, accident/personal injury, and family law matters from intake through resolution
	Conduct consultations and communicate effectively with prospective clients to convert leads into retained cases.
	Supervise and review submitted tasks, case work, and team assignments for accuracy and timeliness.
	Communicate professionally with clients, colleagues, courts, medical providers, experts, agencies, and third parties.
	Draft, review, and file legal documents, pleadings, correspondence, and case materials.
	Maintain organized case management and ensure deadlines are met.
	Assist in developing workflows, improving office efficiency, and supporting firm operations.
	Participate in rigorous training and demonstrate the ability to quickly absorb and apply new information.
	Take initiative in problem-solving and independently follow through on assigned responsibilities.
	Provide leadership, mentorship, and accountability to support staff and team members.
	Lead by example through professionalism, responsiveness, integrity, and dedication to client service


Qualifications/ Requirements


	Foreign Bar license and LLB from an accredited law school ; LLM (not mandatory).
	Strong communication, interpersonal, and client service skills.
	Ability to multitask and manage a high-volume caseload.
	Strong organizational and leadership abilities.
	Fast learner with the ability to adapt in a fast-paced environment.
	Professional, dependable, and detail-oriented.
	Self-motivated, proactive, and solution-oriented.
	Ability to work collaboratively while also handling responsibilities independently.
	Prior experience in immigration, personal injury, or family law is preferred but not required for the right candidate willing to train aggressively.
	Must be coachable and open to intensive training.
	Must be eloquent and confident communicator.


Compensation &amp; Benefits


	Salary: N450,000 monthly
	Performance and leadership growth opportunities.
	Training and mentorship.
	Opportunity for advancement into higher supervisory and management roles.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: cvs@odixcityconsulting.com.ng using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.",

  "validThrough": "2026-07-01",

    "@type": "Organization",

  },

    "@type": "Place",

      "@type": "PostalAddress",
       "postalCode": ". ",

Legal Assistant Job at Odixcity Consulting




        
    

        
        Odixcity Consulting - Our client, a growing Practice, is recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Legal Assistant 

Job Location: Port Harcourt, Rivers (Remote)
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	We are seeking a motivated, eloquent, and highly responsible Legal Assistant to join our growing practice.
	This role is ideal for an attorney who is hardworking, proactive, eager to learn, and ready to step into a leadership and supervisory position within the firm.
	As a legal Assistant you will handle assigned matters in immigration, personal injury/accident, and family law cases while also assisting with team supervision, client intake conversion, task review, and operational support.
	The ideal candidate must be able to undergo rigorous training, adapt quickly, and lead by example through professionalism, accountability, and strong work ethics.


Responsibilities


	Handle assigned immigration, accident/personal injury, and family law matters from intake through resolution
	Conduct consultations and communicate effectively with prospective clients to convert leads into retained cases.
	Supervise and review submitted tasks, case work, and team assignments for accuracy and timeliness.
	Communicate professionally with clients, colleagues, courts, medical providers, experts, agencies, and third parties.
	Draft, review, and file legal documents, pleadings, correspondence, and case materials.
	Maintain organized case management and ensure deadlines are met.
	Assist in developing workflows, improving office efficiency, and supporting firm operations.
	Participate in rigorous training and demonstrate the ability to quickly absorb and apply new information.
	Take initiative in problem-solving and independently follow through on assigned responsibilities.
	Provide leadership, mentorship, and accountability to support staff and team members.
	Lead by example through professionalism, responsiveness, integrity, and dedication to client service


Qualifications/ Requirements


	Foreign Bar license and LLB from an accredited law school ; LLM (not mandatory).
	Strong communication, interpersonal, and client service skills.
	Ability to multitask and manage a high-volume caseload.
	Strong organizational and leadership abilities.
	Fast learner with the ability to adapt in a fast-paced environment.
	Professional, dependable, and detail-oriented.
	Self-motivated, proactive, and solution-oriented.
	Ability to work collaboratively while also handling responsibilities independently.
	Prior experience in immigration, personal injury, or family law is preferred but not required for the right candidate willing to train aggressively.
	Must be coachable and open to intensive training.
	Must be eloquent and confident communicator.


Compensation & Benefits


	Salary: N450,000 monthly
	Performance and leadership growth opportunities.
	Training and mentorship.
	Opportunity for advancement into higher supervisory and management roles.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.    

    

        Click here to Apply  
        

    
     




        
        

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});

  "description": "Talenture Group specializes in Financial Services, Technology and Telecoms recruitment, delivering innovative solutions to clients. We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Pschometrican / Psychologist
Job Location: Lekki Phase 1, Lagos
Employment Type: Contract

Job Summary


	We are seeking a qualified and certified Psychometrician/Psychologist to join our team. The ideal candidate will be responsible for conducting end-to-end candidate assessments, administering psychometric evaluations, interpreting assessment results and preparing comprehensive reports for clients and internal stakeholders.
	The successful candidate must have experience working with both corporate clients and candidates within a professional setting and possess strong analytical, communication and reporting skills.


Key Responsibilities


	Conduct end-to-end psychometric and behavioural assessments for candidates
	Administer aptitude, personality, cognitive, and behavioural assessment tools
	Interpret assessment results and prepare detailed assessment reports
	Present and explain assessment findings to clients and stakeholders professionally
	Recommend suitable candidates based on assessment outcomes
	Ensure proper documentation and confidentiality of assessment records
	Work closely with recruitment and HR teams on talent evaluation processes
	Provide insights and recommendations to improve hiring decisions
	Stay updated on industry trends, psychometric tools, and assessment methodologies.


Requirements


	Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree in Psychology or related fields
	Minimum of 2 years of relevant experience in a corporate or consulting environment
	Professional certification in Psychometrics or related assessment discipline is mandatory
	Proven experience conducting candidate assessments and preparing assessment reports
	Strong knowledge of psychometric tools, assessment platforms and evaluation methodologies
	Experience working directly with clients and candidates in a professional setting
	Excellent analytical, report writing and presentation skills
	Strong communication and interpersonal abilities
	Must reside within commutable distance to Lekki, Lagos.

Pschometrican / Psychologist Job at Talenture Group




        
    

        
        Talenture Group specializes in Financial Services, Technology and Telecoms recruitment, delivering innovative solutions to clients. With a focus on expanding our dynamic sales team, we aim to drive growth within the commercial banking sector as a leading provider of cutting-edge workforce solution.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Pschometrican / Psychologist
Job Location: Lekki Phase 1, Lagos
Employment Type: Contract

Job Summary

	We are seeking a qualified and certified Psychometrician/Psychologist to join our team. The ideal candidate will be responsible for conducting end-to-end candidate assessments, administering psychometric evaluations, interpreting assessment results and preparing comprehensive reports for clients and internal stakeholders.
	The successful candidate must have experience working with both corporate clients and candidates within a professional setting and possess strong analytical, communication and reporting skills.


Key Responsibilities


	Conduct end-to-end psychometric and behavioural assessments for candidates
	Administer aptitude, personality, cognitive, and behavioural assessment tools
	Interpret assessment results and prepare detailed assessment reports
	Present and explain assessment findings to clients and stakeholders professionally
	Recommend suitable candidates based on assessment outcomes
	Ensure proper documentation and confidentiality of assessment records
	Work closely with recruitment and HR teams on talent evaluation processes
	Provide insights and recommendations to improve hiring decisions
	Stay updated on industry trends, psychometric tools, and assessment methodologies.


Requirements


	Bachelor’s Degree in Psychology or related fields
	Minimum of 2 years of relevant experience in a corporate or consulting environment
	Professional certification in Psychometrics or related assessment discipline is mandatory
	Proven experience conducting candidate assessments and preparing assessment reports
	Strong knowledge of psychometric tools, assessment platforms and evaluation methodologies
	Experience working directly with clients and candidates in a professional setting
	Excellent analytical, report writing and presentation skills
	Strong communication and interpersonal abilities
	Must reside within commutable distance to Lekki, Lagos.
